sources_audited.note: The 2022 NAICS Manual defines 621498 as medical-staffed establishments providing general or specialized outpatient care, lists the cited community health, pain therapy, sleep disorder, and biofeedback examples, and supplies the relevant cross-references and exclusions.

The May 2023 OEWS table for NAICS 621400 reports the cited 41.13% practitioner/technical, 11.19% healthcare-support, and 15.71% office/administrative shares and the named billing, reception, secretarial, and records occupations. The packet correctly labels the broader-industry mapping as a proxy.

The 2026 AMA survey reports 72% incorporating at least one AI use case, leading documentation and summarization uses, the listed drafting and assistive use cases, and the stated privacy, validation, liability, training, and clinical-judgment constraints. The packet discloses that physician use is not wage displacement or six-digit implementation evidence.

Gallup reports that 22% of employer-business owners planned to sell or transfer ownership in the next five years in its fall 2024 survey. The packet appropriately distinguishes intentions from completed 621498 control transfers.

GAO documents physician-practice mergers, acquisitions, affiliations, hospital, insurer, corporate, and private-equity channels, MSO and roll-up structures, below-threshold transaction reporting gaps, specialty variation, and commercial price effects. The packet discloses that physician practices are adjacent to rather than identical with 621498 centers.

The BLS 2024-2034 industry projections table reports NAICS 621400 employment of 1,115.1 thousand rising to 1,324.8 thousand, 18.8% growth, and real output of $217.8 billion rising to $307.4 billion at 3.5% annually. The packet clearly treats this broader-industry forecast as a proxy.

The current HRSA page supports more than 32.4 million patients, 139.4 million visits in 2024, coordinated services, chronic-disease management, and preventive screening. It does not display the packet's nearly 1,400 organizations, more than 16,000 sites, or 28.6 million patients in 2020 statistics.

The March 2026 CMS guide states payment at 80% of the lesser of FQHC charges or the geographically and otherwise adjusted encounter-based PPS rate, annual market-basket updating, and the 2026 base payment rate of $207.72.

caveats: S7 does not substantiate the nearly 1,400 organizations, more than 16,000 sites, or 28.6 million patients in 2020 statistics used in the e and d5 rationales. Removing those statistics would not change the CONDITIONAL base tier or VALIDATE_ASSUMPTIONS action: HRSA's supported 2024 patient and visit scale still evidences a large safety-net segment, while BLS independently supports the broader outpatient growth proxy.

The a and rho bases are judgmental transformations of broader-industry occupation mix and physician-reported AI use, not direct measures of exposed wages or implemented labor substitution in NAICS 621498. The PROXY labels, bridges, and ranges disclose this limitation, and an evidence-state downgrade would not change the already MODEL_CONDITIONED action.

The s5 and q sources establish adjacent ownership channels, general employer-business intentions, FQHC payment mechanics, and consolidation-related prices, but they do not directly measure completed 621498 control transfers or retained AI benefit. The packet explicitly acknowledges both gaps and uses wide ranges.

The six-digit code is unusually heterogeneous, so HRSA safety-net evidence and broad NAICS 621400 evidence may weight community-health activity differently from the LMM commercial pain, sleep, biofeedback, and multispecialty targets. This is coherently disclosed in the lens, bridges, principal weakness, and tier interval.
summary: Mechanics pass and the record is useful, internally coherent, and candid about its estimate-heavy, broad-proxy construction. Seven cited sources support their registered claims and S7 provides substantial but incomplete support. The unsupported S7 statistics are nonmaterial under the frozen test, while the decisive uncertainties in exposure, implementation, transferable firms, retention, and code mix are appropriately labeled and disclosed. Publish with caveats.
